title: Oracle Developer Coaching Event April 29: 'Next-Level OCI' — CI Compose, OCDK, and VS Code for AI Database
summary: On April 29, Oracle hosted the Developer Coaching event 'Next-Level OCI: Enhancing Developer Productivity with CI Compose, OCDK, and VS Code' — a live session featuring Kris Rice (SVP, Software Development, Oracle AI Database) and Jeff Smith (Distinguished Product Manager, Oracle AI Database). CI Compose provides declarative multi-service pipeline definitions inside OCI; OCDK (Oracle Cloud Development Kit) enables Infrastructure as Code for OCI services; and the VS Code Extension delivers direct Oracle AI Database 26ai interaction inside the IDE without leaving the development environment. For teams building Oracle APEX solutions on AI Database 26ai that need a modern CI/CD pipeline, these tools substantially reduce the DevOps cycle from planning to production.

title: Oracle London Advanced Training April 29: Upgrade, Migration and Patching to Oracle AI Database 26ai
summary: On April 29 in London, Oracle delivered a live Advanced Edition training session on 'Upgrade, Migration and Patching to Oracle AI Database 26ai and 19c' for Oracle database administrators and architects. The session covered the AutoUpgrade path from 19c to AI Database 26ai, managing Release Updates mid-lifecycle, OJVM patching, and the Windows platform timeline (ETA May 12, 2026). The London session followed the April 28 release of RU 23.26.2 and RU 19.31, giving DBAs current hands-on guidance immediately after the new patch cycle. For enterprises moving databases from legacy ERP to Oracle AI Database 26ai, Advanced Edition training from Oracle instructors is the safest preparatory step before a production upgrade.

title: Traust Confirms Two Sessions at KScope26 in Denver — Oracle APEX and AI in June 2026
summary: Traust — Oracle consulting partner and North America APEX event organizer — confirmed two technical sessions at ODTUG KScope26 (June 14–18, 2026, Gaylord Rockies Resort, Denver). Traust also organized the April 30 Minneapolis APEX Bootcamp and Executive Roundtable jointly with Oracle and TCOUG. KScope26 is the largest Oracle APEX conference in the US: 250+ technical sessions covering Oracle APEX, OCI, EPM, Analytics, and Database from Oracle Product Management and ACE speakers. For international teams evaluating Oracle APEX as a replacement for legacy ERP, post-conference KScope26 materials represent the most detailed publicly available technical content from the primary Oracle APEX development team.

title: Oracle EBS Tech Blog Confirms: April 2026 Critical Patch Update Now Available for E-Business Suite
summary: The Oracle EBS Tech Blog published confirmation that the April 2026 Critical Patch Update (CPU) is now available for Oracle E-Business Suite — including all security fixes from Oracle's quarterly Security Alert and selected bug-fix patches for EBS components. EBS 12.2 is certified for Oracle AI Database 26ai (confirmed earlier in April), giving EBS customers a secured platform with a clear path to 26ai AI capabilities. Regular quarterly CPUs for Oracle EBS are a concrete indicator of vendor support maturity — enterprises that have moved off banned legacy software now operate on a platform with predictable, auditable security maintenance that legacy systems structurally cannot provide.

title: Oracle AI World 2026: Oracle's Annual AI Flagship Conference — October 25–28, Las Vegas
summary: Oracle AI World 2026 takes place October 25–28 at The Venetian Convention and Expo Center in Las Vegas — Oracle's premier annual conference with 20,000+ attendees. Program includes keynotes from Oracle leadership, product demonstrations, customer case studies, and full-day hands-on bootcamps on Sunday October 25 led by Oracle instructors. Oracle AI World 2026 is expected to be the main stage for announcing next-generation Oracle AI Database capabilities, APEX 26.1 GA, and OCI strategic direction for 2027. KScope26 in Denver (June 14–18) and Oracle AI World (October, Las Vegas) are the two key Oracle events of 2026 for enterprises planning or executing migration from legacy ERP to Oracle APEX and AI Database.

title: Oracle APEX Bootcamp Chicago: Final North America Stop — May 7, 2026 at Willis Tower
summary: On May 7, 2026, the final Oracle APEX Bootcamp of the North America Spring 2026 series takes place at Willis Tower, Chicago (233 S Wacker Dr) — a full day of hands-on Oracle APEX and Generative AI. Free to attend, organized by Oracle, COUG (Chicago Oracle User Group), MOUG, and Viscosity. Program: Oracle APEX Bootcamp (9:30 AM–5:45 PM) covering AI Copilot, APEXlang, and the APEX 26.1 development approach. Chicago is the final stop of a five-city North America tour (Boston, Reston, Minneapolis, Chicago, Guadalajara) before KScope26 in Denver, June 14–18. For development teams migrating from legacy ERP to Oracle APEX, the free Bootcamp offers direct access to Oracle APEX Product Management and hands-on experience with APEX 26.1 capabilities.

title: Oracle Database Release Updates 19.31 and 23.26.2 Ship on Linux — April 2026 Patch Cycle Complete
summary: On April 28, Oracle released the April 2026 Release Updates for Oracle AI Database 26ai (RU 23.26.2) and Oracle Database 19c (RU 19.31) on Linux x86-64 — delivered on the revised schedule after the delay announced April 23. RU 23.26.2 for AI Database 26ai incorporates patches from the April 2026 Critical Patch Update plus bug fixes. Remaining schedule: OJVM Release Update on April 30, Windows platform on May 12. Oracle maintains a transparent public patch calendar even when target dates shift. For enterprises comparing Oracle against banned legacy ERP systems like 1C/BAS, the quarterly patching cycle is a practical vendor maturity criterion: banned legacy software receives no centralized security updates, while Oracle AI Database 26ai receives quarterly CPUs plus out-of-band Security Alerts.

title: Oracle FY2026 Q3: Cloud Revenue +44%, Total Revenue +22%, RPO $553 Billion — AI Infrastructure Transformation Confirmed
summary: On March 10, Oracle announced Q3 FY2026 results: total revenue $17.2 billion (+22% year-over-year), cloud revenue $8.9 billion (+44%). First quarter in 15+ years where both organic total revenue and Non-GAAP EPS grew 20%+ simultaneously. Remaining Performance Obligations (RPO) reached $553 billion (+325% YoY), driven largely by large-scale AI contracts including the $300 billion OpenAI deal starting 2027. FY2027 guidance raised to $90 billion revenue, $50 billion CapEx. For enterprise customers choosing Oracle APEX and AI Database as the platform for ERP modernization, these results provide financial confirmation of Oracle's strategic reliability: the company is transitioning from legacy software vendor to foundational AI infrastructure provider with long-term contractual commitments.

title: Oracle RAC + AI Database 26ai: Linear AI Workload Scaling to 100,000+ Queries/Second Without Code Changes
summary: Oracle published a technical overview of Oracle AI Database 26ai scalability with RAC for enterprise-grade AI workloads. With RAC's share-everything architecture, vector similarity search scales linearly: over 100,000 vector distance queries per second under GloVe workload across four nodes — without any application code changes required. AI inference, similarity search, and AI-native workloads scale transparently as RAC nodes are added. Active-active replication via OCI GoldenGate 26ai delivers sub-3-second failover with zero data loss for agentic workloads. For enterprises migrating from legacy ERP to Oracle APEX with AI analytics, this means growing query volumes are handled by adding RAC nodes — not rearchitecting the application. A fundamental contrast to monolithic legacy ERP, where vertical scaling has hard ceilings and AI integration requires separate external infrastructure.

title: Oracle MySQL HeatWave: MySQL 8.0 Support Extended to April 2027 — Automatic Upgrade to 8.0.46 in June
summary: Oracle announced an extension of MySQL 8.0 support in the OCI HeatWave service until April 2027 — one year beyond the original April 2026 end-of-life date. Existing HeatWave instances running MySQL 8.0 will be automatically updated to version 8.0.46 during the June 2026 maintenance cycle, with critical security patches continuing through the extension. Creating new 8.0 instances is no longer permitted; changing shapes for 8.0 instances is also restricted. After April 2027, any remaining 8.0 instances will be automatically migrated to MySQL 8.4 LTS during their next maintenance window. For enterprises using MySQL HeatWave on OCI that have not yet completed migration to 8.4 LTS, Oracle provides an additional year to plan and execute the upgrade without forced downtime.

title: Industry Analysts Confirm: Oracle AI Database 26ai Leads Mission-Critical Availability in the AI Era
summary: Oracle published a roundup of leading industry analyst perspectives on Oracle AI Database 26ai. Key assessments: 'Oracle is democratizing extreme availability, forcing competitors to rethink their high-availability claims'; Diamond-tier MAA with failover under 3 seconds and zero data loss meets the demands of financial exchanges and mission-critical applications; 'Only Oracle AI Database delivers transactional processing and analytics in a single engine with AES-256 encryption and post-quantum cryptography.' Analysts emphasize that Oracle AI Database 26ai delivers enterprise-grade reliability and security that legacy ERP systems are structurally incapable of matching. For organizations migrating from legacy software, moving to Oracle represents a qualitative leap in platform reliability at a level the previous environment could not approach.

title: Spring AI + Oracle AI Database: Practical Guide to Adding Persistent Memory to AI Agents
summary: The Oracle Developer Blog published a technical walkthrough: 'How I Added Memory to an AI Agent Using Spring AI and Oracle AI Database.' The post demonstrates implementing persistent memory for Spring AI-based agents using Oracle AI Database 26ai as the unified store for all three memory types: episodic (relational table for chat history), semantic (hybrid vector index for domain knowledge retrieval), and procedural (application tables accessed via @Tool methods). Oracle AI Database 26ai stores all three memory types in a single engine — no separate graph database, vector store, or document store required. Agents retain context across sessions without external RAG infrastructure. For Oracle APEX developers building AI-agent applications on Java and Spring, this is a production-ready reference architecture with working code.

title: Oracle APEX 26.1 and Vibe Coding: APEXlang AI Copilot as Oracle's Answer to Generative Low-Code
summary: Oracle confirmed that the Oracle APEX team is adapting 'vibe coding' approaches for APEX 26.1 through APEXlang and AI Copilot. APEXlang is an open application specification in YAML/JSON format with native git version control and CI/CD support. AI Copilot lets developers describe an application in natural language and receive a working APEX project in .apx format — with a transparent intermediate review step for the AI-generated result. Oracle positions APEXlang as the foundation of next-generation generative low-code development: the key differentiator from classic vibe coding is human-readable, auditable output that maintains enterprise control. The roadmap is officially confirmed at apex.oracle.com/roadmap. For teams migrating from legacy ERP to Oracle APEX and planning APEX 26.1 adoption, this defines the strategic development direction for the years ahead.

title: Oracle AI Database 26ai On-Premises: GA Confirmed for Linux x86-64, Windows and AIX Planned for 2026
summary: Oracle confirmed the on-premises deployment roadmap for Oracle AI Database 26ai outside the Autonomous Cloud. GA for Linux x86-64 was released in January 2026 (RU 23.26.1) as Enterprise Edition with the full AI feature set: AI Vector Search, SQL FILTER, OML4Py/OML4SQL API v2, Private Agent Factory, and True Cache. Releases for Windows and AIX are scheduled for later in calendar year 2026. Oracle is covering all three major server platforms where legacy ERP environments commonly still run. For enterprises migrating from legacy systems who want to adopt AI Database 26ai without moving to the cloud, Oracle is providing a clear on-premises path: Linux is already available, Windows and AIX are next. Details are confirmed in MOS Note 742060.1 — Release Schedule of Current Database Releases.

title: Oracle APEX Bootcamp & Executive Roundtable in Minneapolis — Final North America Stop, April 30
summary: On April 30, 2026, Traust, Oracle, and the Twin Cities Oracle User Group (TCOUG) host the final Oracle APEX Bootcamp of the North America 2026 series at McNamara Alumni Center, University of Minnesota, Minneapolis. The day includes two blocks: Executive Roundtable (7:30–9:00 AM) featuring a presentation from Oracle APEX Product Management on the platform's strategic direction and APEX 26.1; and Oracle APEX Bootcamp (10:30 AM–6:00 PM), a hands-on introduction to building APEX applications. Both sessions are free but seating is limited. Next major event: KScope26 in Denver, June 14–18, with a three-day APEX Bootcamp and 250+ technical sessions from Oracle Product Management.

title: Oracle Database Monthly News April 2026: Monthly Digest of Key Updates from CoreTec Oracle Blog
summary: The Oracle CoreTec Blog published the Oracle Database Monthly News for April 2026 — a monthly digest of quick links covering all key Oracle Database updates from the month. Highlights include: online upgrade of Autonomous AI Database from 19c to 26ai in approximately 15 minutes with no extended maintenance window; new Platinum and Diamond availability tiers for 26ai on Exadata; EBS 12.2 certified for Oracle AI Database 26ai; revised Release Update release dates for 19.31 and 23.26.2 (Linux: April 28, OJVM RU: April 30, Windows: May 12); Critical Patch Update April 2026 with 483 patches. The CoreTec Oracle Blog publishes this monthly digest at the end of each month — a practical navigation guide for DBAs, architects, and teams planning migration to Oracle AI Database 26ai.

title: Related Digital Closes $16 Billion Financing for Oracle Data Center Campus in Michigan
summary: On April 25, 2026, Related Digital announced the successful closing of $16 billion in financing for a large-scale data center campus in Saline Township, Michigan — purpose-built for Oracle as part of the Stargate project. The financing includes equity from Related Digital and Blackstone-affiliated funds, plus fixed-rate long-term debt anchored by PIMCO-managed funds. Bank of America served as structuring agent; Goldman Sachs and Wells Fargo also advised. The 'Barn' campus will feature three facilities with over 1 gigawatt of capacity, closed-loop cooling, and LEED certification — construction is already underway. The project will create 2,500+ union construction jobs and 450+ permanent on-site positions. The deal confirms the scale of Oracle's AI infrastructure investment as part of its OpenAI partnership ($300B contract starting 2027) and its broader plan to raise $45–50 billion in capital, of which $30 billion has already been secured.

title: Wedbush Initiates Oracle with 'Outperform' and $225 Target — ORCL Down 24% in 2026, Analysts Say Buy
summary: On April 24, Wedbush Securities initiated coverage of Oracle (ORCL) with an 'Outperform' rating and a 12-month price target of $225 — implying roughly 30% upside from current levels. Senior analyst Dan Ives argues the market is 'fundamentally misinterpreting' Oracle's massive capital expenditures as a gamble rather than a strategic necessity. Oracle is transforming from a legacy software vendor into a foundational AI infrastructure provider backed by long-term contracts, including a $300 billion deal with OpenAI set to begin in 2027. ORCL shares are down roughly 24% in 2026 amid concerns about CapEx scale. Oracle has already raised $30 billion of its planned $45–50 billion capital raise through investment-grade bonds and mandatory convertible preferred stock.

title: OCI A4 Standard Acceleron: New-Generation ARM Instances for AI Inference and Cloud-Native Workloads
summary: Oracle announced OCI A4 Standard Acceleron — the next evolution of ARM-based cloud instances in the Oracle Acceleron family, combining AmpereOne M processors with Oracle's purpose-built Acceleron architecture. Designed for AI inference, cloud-native applications, and enterprise workloads, the new shapes deliver improved efficiency and scalability. Across the entire Acceleron family, OCPU-hour pricing is up to 70% lower than the previous generation. A4 Standard Acceleron joins the already-announced X12 Standard Acceleron (Intel Xeon 6) and E6 Acceleron (storage-optimized shapes). For Oracle APEX applications and enterprise platforms moving from on-premises legacy ERP to OCI, the new Acceleron instances offer higher performance at lower cost — no application code changes required.

title: Oracle AI Database Live Training JAPAC: Free 3-Day Live Sessions April 28–30 — Direct Q&A with Oracle Product Team
summary: Oracle is running the Oracle AI Database Live Training for JAPAC (Japan, Australia, India, and surrounding regions) on April 28–30, 2026 — a free three-day live format with direct Q&A access to Oracle Product Management and engineering teams. Topics cover new Oracle AI Database 26ai capabilities, AI Agent Framework, OML4Py/OML4SQL API v2 with improved ML job tracking and ONNX model support, and the new FILTER clause for aggregate and window functions. A parallel Americas/EMEA session took place April 21–23. For developers building Oracle APEX solutions on AI Database 26ai, this is a direct channel to Oracle engineering — live interaction, not recordings.

title: Oracle Autonomous AI Database: Online Upgrade from 19c to 26ai in ~15 Minutes — No Full Downtime
summary: Oracle published the full process for online upgrade of Autonomous AI Database from Oracle Database 19c to Oracle AI Database 26ai. For read-only workloads — analytics and reporting — the upgrade runs with minimal disruption: approximately 15 minutes in read-only mode instead of a lengthy maintenance window. The underlying architecture makes it possible: compute and storage are decoupled in Autonomous Database, so Oracle switches to a pre-provisioned compute stack already running the 26ai engine instead of traditional in-place binary patching. For read-write workloads, a GoldenGate replication-based upgrade path is available with near-zero downtime. The entire process is a single click in the OCI Console. A refreshable clone-based upgrade is also available for parallel testing of applications on 26ai with continuous data sync from the 19c primary. For enterprises planning to move legacy ERP data to Oracle AI Database 26ai, this removes a key technical obstacle — the extended maintenance window typically required for database upgrades.

title: OCI HeatWave MySQL 9.6.0 Now Supported: Auto-Materialized View Substitution and Grouping Sets for Analytics
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure HeatWave MySQL added support for MySQL 9.6.0 (Innovation), 8.4.8 (LTS), and 8.0.45 (Bugfix) — all three production-grade versions with security and bug fixes included. New DB systems now default to version 8.4.8. Among the new analytical capabilities: auto-materialized view substitution, which automatically selects the optimal materialized view for a query without any application code changes; and Grouping Sets, a SQL operator for aggregating data across multiple dimensions in a single query instead of separate UNION statements. MySQL 9.6.0 belongs to the Innovation series — carrying the latest MySQL features between LTS releases. For analytics platforms and enterprise solutions built on Oracle Cloud, MySQL 9.6 enables more efficient execution of complex OLAP queries without rebuilding existing SQL logic.

title: Oracle APEX: Foundations Learning Path — Free 3+ Hour Official Course from the Oracle APEX Product Team
summary: Oracle University published the Oracle APEX: Foundations Learning Path — a free official training course of 3+ hours delivered by the Oracle APEX Product Management Team. Designed for beginners and those new to Oracle APEX, the path covers App Builder, SQL Workshop, building cloud applications on APEX with OCI, workspace provisioning, and managing reports and forms. Upon completion, participants receive an official Oracle badge. The course is available at no cost on mylearn.oracle.com. For organizations evaluating Oracle APEX as a replacement for legacy ERP systems and looking to upskill their developers, the free official learning path provides a practical first step with no external training costs.

title: OCI Karpenter Provider (KPO) Goes GA: Smart Kubernetes Autoscaling with Just-in-Time Node Provisioning
summary: Oracle announced general availability of the Karpenter Provider for OCI (KPO) — an open-source Kubernetes node autoscaler that provisions the right OCI compute shape in real time based on workload demand, replacing static predefined node pools. Key benefits: just-in-time provisioning driven by actual pod scheduling needs, fewer manual node pool configurations, a wider mix of OCI compute shapes in a single policy, automatic consolidation of empty or underused nodes to reduce costs, and simplified scaling across different shapes and architectures. Kubernetes 1.31 or later is required. For teams deploying Oracle APEX and enterprise applications with Kubernetes orchestration on OCI, KPO significantly simplifies resource management under variable load without manual configuration.

title: Oracle Database April 2026 Release Updates 19.31 and 23.26.2 Delayed to April 28
summary: On April 23, Oracle DBA Mike Dietrich confirmed on his blog that the April 2026 Oracle Database Release Updates 19.31 and 23.26.2 for Linux have been delayed — the new release date is April 28, 2026. The OJVM Release Update 19.31 is expected on April 30. The Windows release has been pushed to May 12. Oracle has not publicly stated the reason for the delay. Database administrators are advised to update their patch cycle schedules accordingly. Details are confirmed in Oracle Support Knowledge Base KB106822 — Primary Note for Database Quarterly Release Updates.

title: Oracle AI Database Agent for Gemini Enterprise (Preview): Query Oracle Data in Natural Language — Google Cloud Next 2026
summary: At Google Cloud Next 2026 on April 22, Oracle announced the Oracle AI Database Agent for Gemini Enterprise — now available in preview on Google Cloud Marketplace. Enterprise users can ask natural language questions against Oracle AI Database through Gemini Enterprise without writing SQL: the agent applies semantic understanding and governance guardrails, returning answers grounded in live Oracle data without extracting it externally. Full Agent-to-Agent (A2A) compatibility enables automated multi-step workflows. Oracle also announced that Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud is adding OCI GoldenGate Service (preview) for near real-time Oracle data replication to Google BigQuery, plus integration with Knowledge Catalog and Database Center; the service is expanding to 20 regions.

title: Oracle Select AI Pre-Built Agents: Ready-to-Use AI Agents for Enterprise Data Analysis in Autonomous AI Database
summary: Oracle introduced a suite of pre-built AI agents based on the new Select AI Agent Framework for Oracle Autonomous AI Database. The Database Knowledge Agent translates natural language prompts into SQL to retrieve structured facts and entities without writing code. The Structured Data Analysis Agent analyzes tabular data (SQL tables, CSV files) using Python pandas to build charts, spot trends, and flag anomalies. The Deep Data Research Agent handles complex research tasks by iterating across documents and web sources. The Data Science Agent delivers a conversational ML experience covering the full machine learning lifecycle directly inside the database. Select AI Translate has been expanded to support Google, AWS, and Azure as additional translation providers.

title: OCI X12 Standard Acceleron Compute: New Oracle Cloud Instances Powered by Intel Xeon 6 — Up to 2.5x Faster Than X9 Standard
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure launched OCI X12 Standard Acceleron Compute — a new generation of cloud instances based on Intel Xeon 6900 P-cores, replacing X9 Standard (Intel Xeon 3). Performance: 1.5x average improvement over X9 Standard for general compute workloads, up to 2.5x for compute-intensive bare metal workloads, 42% higher memory bandwidth. Bare metal: 120 Intel Xeon 6 cores, 1.15 TB RAM, up to 100 Gbps networking. VM shapes: 1 to 78 OCPUs, up to 720 GB RAM, up to 100 Gbps. Pricing: $0.0119/OCPU-hour and $0.0114/GB-hour. For Oracle APEX applications and enterprises moving on-premises workloads to OCI, X12 delivers significant performance gains with no application code changes required.

title: Oracle ACE Monthly Product Pulse April 2026: AI Database Live Training & SQL FILTER in 26ai
summary: The Oracle ACE Program published its April 2026 Monthly Product Pulse with practical developer updates across the Oracle ecosystem. Highlights: Oracle AI Database Live Training April 21–23 (Americas/EMEA) and April 28–30 (JAPAC) — three days of live Q&A with Oracle PM and development teams. New OML4Py/OML4SQL API v2 with improved ML job tracking and support for larger ONNX models for embedded machine learning. A new FILTER clause for aggregate and window functions makes SQL queries more expressive without complex subqueries.

title: KScope 26: Largest Oracle APEX Conference in the US — June 14–18, 2026, Denver, Colorado
summary: ODTUG KScope 26 takes place June 14–18, 2026 at Gaylord Rockies Resort & Convention Center in Aurora (Denver), Colorado. The largest Oracle APEX conference in the United States: 250+ technical sessions covering Oracle APEX, OCI, EPM, Analytics, and Database tracks. Sunday Symposiums, hands-on training, and networking with Oracle Product Managers and ACE speakers. A pre-conference Oracle APEX Bootcamp takes place April 30 in Minneapolis (free to attend). Registration is open.

title: Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud at Google Cloud Next 2026: 15 Regions, New Restore Capabilities, and Gemini Session
summary: At Google Cloud Next 2026 (April 22–24, Las Vegas), Oracle announced Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud now spans 15 regions (20 sites) globally — adding 7 new regions across Asia Pacific, Europe, North America, and Latin America. New restore capabilities for Autonomous AI Database give users greater control to recover from data loss or corruption with reduced operational risk. The session 'Talk to your data with Gemini Enterprise' previews an upcoming integration connecting Google Gemini with Oracle AI Database for enterprise data querying.

title: Stargate Michigan: OpenAI and Oracle Sign Union Labor Agreement — 2,500 AI Infrastructure Construction Jobs
summary: On April 20, at NABTU's Legislative Conference in Washington D.C., OpenAI, Oracle, and Related Digital signed a union labor agreement to build the Stargate Michigan data center — the first Stargate site committed to union-built construction under an agreement with NABTU, expected to create 2,500 skilled trades jobs. The flagship Abilene, Texas campus is already live with 1.2 GW of Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, with construction underway across additional US states. Total program investment exceeds $400 billion with planned capacity approaching 7 GW.

title: Oracle Health Achieves CMS Aligned Network Status and Launches 'Kill the Clipboard' Digital Check-In
summary: On April 20, Oracle Health announced it achieved CMS Aligned Network status and launched a solution supporting CMS's 'Kill the Clipboard' initiative. Leveraging CLEAR, the solution enables patients to digitally verify their identity and share health records at check-in — eliminating redundant paperwork and manual data entry. The announcement reinforces Oracle Health's leadership in healthcare interoperability and its commitment to reducing administrative burden in clinical settings.

title: Oracle at Google Cloud Next 2026: Gemini and Oracle AI Database — New AI Integration for Enterprise Data
summary: At Google Cloud Next 2026, Oracle and Google are presenting a joint AI strategy: Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud enables querying enterprise Oracle data through Google Gemini in the 'Talk to your data with Gemini Enterprise' session. Oracle APEX developers will be able to integrate Gemini with Oracle AI Database without moving data outside a secured perimeter. The Oracle-Google Cloud partnership now spans 15 regions, with Oracle Database deployed natively in Google Cloud data centers as a jointly managed service.

title: Oracle Critical Patch Update April 2026: 483 New Security Patches for Database, ORDS, and GoldenGate
summary: On April 21, Oracle released its quarterly Critical Patch Update (CPU) for April 2026 — 483 new security patches. Oracle Database received 8 patches, 4 of which are remotely exploitable without authentication; affected versions include 19c (19.3–19.30), 21c (21.3–21.21), and AI Database 26ai (23.4–23.26.1). Oracle REST Data Services: 2 patches (both remote, CVSS 7.5). GoldenGate: 10 patches, 7 remote. Oracle strongly recommends applying patches as soon as possible. Quarterly CPUs represent a fundamental difference from legacy systems like 1C/BAS, which receive no centralized security patches after being banned.

title: Oracle APEX April 2026 Newsletter: Agentic AI with Oracle AI Database and the Acordia Enterprise Case
summary: The April 2026 Oracle APEX Newsletter features a deep dive on Agentic AI with Oracle AI Database — how 26ai and APEX enable agentic automation for business applications without external AI infrastructure. The exclusive Acordia case study (founder Vincent Lockyer) shows an enterprise data lifecycle management solution built on Oracle APEX and OCI. Also included: new ACE community blog picks and upcoming spring events — APEX Connect 2026 at Heide Park (May 18) and the final North America Bootcamp stops.

title: OCI Database Migration Service Gets Assessment Feature — Automated Readiness Check Before Migration
summary: OCI Database Migration Service introduced the Assessment feature: automated analysis of an existing database, recommendation of the optimal migration method, and schema and user readiness validation. The feature reduces the risk of unexpected incompatibilities during migration and enables a technical audit before the project begins. For organizations planning a move from legacy ERP to Oracle, the automated assessment provides a practical first step without engaging consultants at the initial stage.

title: Oracle Database Cloud BYOL: Move Existing Oracle Licenses to OCI Without Repurchasing
summary: Oracle published an updated guide on BYOL (Bring Your Own License) strategy for Oracle Database Cloud on OCI. Customers with existing Oracle Database EE or SE2 licenses can bring them to OCI at no additional licensing cost using Authorized Cloud Environments. This significantly reduces migration costs for enterprises that already have on-premises Oracle Database licenses. For organizations that have already invested in Oracle Database while migrating away from legacy ERP, BYOL removes a key financial barrier to moving workloads to OCI.

title: APEX AI Interactive Reports: Query Your Report in Natural Language — Millions of Existing IRs Become AI-Ready in APEX 26.1
summary: Oracle published 'Talk to Your Data, Trust the Result. Introducing APEX AI Interactive Reports.' The key APEX 26.1 advantage: any existing Interactive Report becomes AI-enabled after a platform upgrade — no application code changes required. An LLM converts natural language questions into SQL filters and aggregates that users can see and edit directly for full transparency. A built-in chat mode is also available within the IR component. For enterprise analytics teams on Oracle APEX, this means instant AI enrichment of all existing reports without rebuilding them.

title: Oracle AI Database Private Agent Factory: No-Code Platform for Enterprise AI Agents on Corporate Data
summary: On March 24, Oracle introduced Private Agent Factory — a no-code component of Oracle AI Database 26ai for building and deploying trusted AI agents. Agents are built on private corporate data with full audit trail and security controls, without extracting data externally. The platform supports dynamic agentic workflows, external REST API integration, and MCP server connections. Finished agents deploy as portable containers and integrate with Oracle AI Agent Memory — a persistent agent memory layer built into the database.

title: Oracle AI Agent Memory: Unified Persistent Agent Memory Built Directly Into the Database
summary: Oracle introduced the Unified Memory Core — a built-in persistent memory engine for AI agents in Oracle AI Database 26ai. Rather than maintaining separate graph, document, and vector stores, a single unified layer handles all types of agent memory: short-term (session context), long-term (facts and preferences), and semantic (vector associations). AI Agent Memory integrates with Private Agent Factory and enables agents to 'remember' across sessions without external RAG infrastructure. Expected availability: 2026.

title: Oracle EBS 12.2 Certified for Oracle AI Database 26ai on On-Premises Linux x86-64
summary: On April 7, Oracle confirmed certification of Oracle E-Business Suite Release 12.2 for use with Oracle AI Database 26ai (RU 23.26.1) on on-premises Linux x86-64. EBS 12.2 customers on database 19c get a direct upgrade path to 26ai via the AutoUpgrade utility — no application-layer changes required. Alongside certification, Oracle introduced the EDBPC (EBS Database Parameter Checker) utility to validate database parameters before migration. For enterprises planning a move to Oracle AI Database, this removes a key technical barrier.

title: OCI: Oracle Container Engine Supports Kubernetes 1.35 'Timbernetes' — In-Place Pod Resource Updates
summary: Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE) added support for Kubernetes 1.35, codenamed 'Timbernetes.' Key highlights: in-place CPU/memory resource updates for Pods without restarting — significant for stateful and restart-sensitive workloads; improved scheduling and security foundations. Clusters on Oracle Linux 7 must migrate to OL8+ since Kubernetes 1.35 requires cgroup v2. For enterprise teams building Oracle APEX applications with Kubernetes orchestration, this version enables finer-grained resource tuning without service interruptions.

title: Oracle Database@AWS Now Available in 12 Global Regions: Frankfurt, Tokyo, Ohio and More
summary: Oracle Database@AWS expanded to 12 AWS regions worldwide. New regions include Frankfurt (EU-Central-1), Tokyo (AP-Northeast-1), Ohio (US-East-2), London (EU-West-2), Mumbai (AP-South-1), Hyderabad (AP-South-2), and Seoul (AP-Northeast-2). The service lets customers run Oracle Exadata and Oracle RAC natively inside AWS — without moving data to OCI. European and Asia-Pacific organizations with in-region data residency requirements now have full support. For businesses migrating from legacy ERP to Oracle, this eliminates the 'we're already on AWS' objection.

title: OCI Cache Valkey 8.1: New Release with JSON Module and Vector Similarity Search
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Cache updated to Valkey 8.1. The new release includes a native JSON module, valkey-search for high-performance vector similarity search, improved I/O threading (up to 3.5x faster for KEYS operations and replication), an optimized hash table with reduced per-key memory usage, and iterator prefetching. For Oracle APEX applications using semantic search and RAG scenarios, Valkey 8.1 enables vector search directly in the cache layer — no additional AI infrastructure required.

title: Oracle + Bloom Energy: 2.8 GW Fuel Cell Deal for OCI AI Data Centers — Grid-Independent Cloud Power
summary: On April 14, Bloom Energy and Oracle announced an expanded strategic partnership: Oracle will procure up to 2.8 gigawatts of solid oxide fuel cell (SOFC) systems to power its AI data centers in the US. An initial 1.2 GW is already contracted and being deployed. Bloom delivered the first system in 55 days — 35 days ahead of the expected 90-day schedule. The fuel cells operate independently of the electrical grid and support high-density AI workloads. Market reaction: Bloom Energy stock surged 22%.

title: APEX Alpe Adria 2026: Oracle APEX Community Conference Held in Ljubljana
summary: On April 17, APEX Alpe Adria 2026 took place at Four Points by Sheraton Ljubljana — the annual Oracle APEX community conference organized by Slovenia, Croatia, and Austria. Key topics: APEXlang and the .apx format for DevOps-native development, APEX 26.1 AI Copilot, next-generation Interactive Reports, and Map Regions. The event reinforces growing Central European interest in Oracle APEX and the strengthening ecosystem around APEX 26.1 as an enterprise low-code platform.

title: Oracle and AWS Expand Multicloud Networking: Private Managed Connection Between OCI and AWS
summary: On April 16, Oracle and AWS announced expanded multicloud networking collaboration. Enterprise customers will gain a fast, private, managed connection between OCI and AWS — without separate network providers or physical infrastructure. Built on Oracle Interconnect and AWS Interconnect, supporting full-stack and split-stack multicloud deployments. First availability: AWS US East (N. Virginia). OCI is already interconnected with 26 partner cloud regions.

title: Oracle APEX 26.1: AI in Interactive Reports, Pattern Pages & New Universal Theme — Community Review
summary: The Into The Oracle Verse blog published a detailed review of APEX 26.1 innovations. AI in Interactive Reports lets users query data tables in natural language — the system auto-applies filters, builds charts, and responds in a chat interface. Pattern Pages simplify UI pattern reuse across large teams. The new Universal Theme adds advanced mobile features. APEXlang AI Copilot generates complete applications from a plain-text description.

title: Oracle AI Database 26ai: Platinum and Diamond Availability Tiers for Mission-Critical Workloads
summary: On April 9, Oracle announced new availability tiers for Oracle AI Database 26ai on Exadata. Platinum tier delivers failover in under 30 seconds for Active Data Guard and RAC — no application changes required. Diamond tier combines Exadata, RAC, Active Data Guard, and Zero Data Loss Recovery for stock-exchange-level availability, with no manual intervention needed. Up to 4x faster failover for large, complex deployments — enterprise-grade mission-critical availability without specialized in-house expertise.

title: Oracle Data Deep Dive NYC: AI Database as the Foundation for Agentic AI — A New Architectural Paradigm
summary: On April 15, Oracle held Oracle Data Deep Dive NYC in New York — an event focused on its AI database convergence strategy for enterprise agentic AI. Oracle bets on a unified engine approach: vector search, graph, JSON, relational, and agent memory — without purpose-built AI silos. Exadata Exascale uses Raft replication for sub-3-second zero-data-loss failover designed specifically for agentic workloads. Oracle challenges the 'orchestration above data' paradigm: agent logic should execute as close to the data as possible, not above fragmented data stores.

title: Oracle EPM April 2026 (26.04): Fusion AI Planning Agent — From Quarterly Cycles to Event-Driven FP&A
summary: Oracle's April 2026 Fusion Cloud EPM update (26.04) integrates Fusion AI Agents into financial planning workflows. The Planning Agent shifts FP&A teams from quarterly cycles to event-driven planning: real-time variance and trend analysis via natural language, what-if simulations on Fusion ERP/EPM data, and automated narrative reporting. New REST APIs enable programmatic agentic AI interaction with EPM Cloud. Predictive Cash Forecasting — previously available only in beta — is now open to all customers.

title: Qualys VMDR & TotalCloud Now on Oracle Cloud Marketplace: One-Click OCI Security
summary: On April 15, Qualys announced availability of VMDR (Vulnerability Management, Detection and Response) and TotalCloud CNAPP on the Oracle Cloud Marketplace. The solution delivers unified visibility across OCI cloud, containers, workloads, and applications with risk-based vulnerability prioritization. One-click provisioning via native OCI integration. Customers can use existing OCI committed spend — no separate procurement process required.

title: OCI Java Management Service: JDK 26 Support — Oracle First Public Cloud Provider
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Java Management Service added support for JDK 26, released March 17, 2026. OCI became the first public cloud provider to offer Oracle JDK 26 — featuring AI integration, new security tooling, and performance improvements. Oracle also announced the Java Verified Portfolio (JVP) — a curated set of Oracle-supported tools, frameworks, and libraries, free for Java SE subscribers and OCI customers running Java workloads on OCI.

title: Oracle Financial Services Extends Agentic AI Platform to Corporate Banking: Agents for Loans, Guarantees & SCF
summary: At the Oracle Financial Services Summit on April 14, Oracle expanded its agentic AI platform to corporate banking. New pre-built AI agents cover the full cycle: Loan Data Extraction Agent processes lengthy credit agreements, Financial Data Validation Agent verifies financial inputs, Application Validator Agent handles bank guarantee applications, and SCF Program Creation Agent launches supply chain finance programs. Built with human-in-the-loop governance. Oracle plans 100+ corporate and retail banking agents within 12 months.

title: Oracle Primavera Unifier Gets AI Agents for Capital Project Management — Oracle Stock +6.5%
summary: On April 14, Oracle announced an AI overhaul for Primavera Unifier, its flagship capital project management platform. New capabilities: AI workflow summaries, no-code/low-code process automation via Oracle Integration, real-time adapters and triggers, improved reporting dashboards, and audit-ready compliance. Oracle Integration gives AI agents secure, fully auditable access to enterprise data. Markets responded with Oracle stock surging 6.5% on April 14, 2026.

title: Oracle AI World Tour Singapore: SMRT Deploys OCI Enterprise AI for Rail Maintenance Intelligence
summary: At Oracle AI World Tour Singapore on April 14, SMRT — Singapore's leading public transportation provider — announced a pilot using OCI Enterprise AI and Oracle Autonomous AI Database. The JARVIS platform (built by STRIDES Technologies) unifies operational data from multiple systems and applies AI for rail infrastructure condition analysis via a generative AI chatbot interface. The case demonstrates Oracle AI applicability in mission-critical industrial infrastructure.

title: Oracle Financial Crime & Compliance: New AI Agents for Suspicious Transaction Detection and Compliance
summary: On April 9, Oracle announced enhancements to the Oracle FCCM (Financial Crime and Compliance Management) platform with Lucinity technology and new AI agents. Agents automate detection of suspicious financial transactions, compliance verification, and transaction monitoring. For banks and financial institutions migrating from legacy systems, Oracle now offers a fully AI-native financial compliance infrastructure.

title: OCI Enterprise AI Goes GA: Oracle Unifies AI Models, Agents, and Governance in One Platform
summary: On March 26, Oracle announced the general availability of OCI Enterprise AI — a platform combining OCI Enterprise AI Models (frontier model access), OCI Enterprise AI Agents (build and deploy agentic workloads using managed tools and open-source frameworks), and OCI Enterprise AI Governance (enterprise-grade security and compliance). OpenAI Responses API compatibility lets teams reuse existing code patterns. On April 15, Oracle hosts an introductory webinar.

title: OCI Compute: AMD Instinct MI355X GPUs — Oracle First Public Hyperscaler
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure became the first public cloud provider to offer AMD Instinct MI355X GPUs. OCI Supercluster scales to 131,072 GPUs. Each MI355X carries 288 GB of HBM3E memory at 8 TB/s bandwidth — nearly 3x more powerful than the previous generation. In Q3 2026, Oracle and AMD plan to deploy an AMD Instinct MI450 Series supercluster with 50,000 GPUs.

title: Ukraine Bill 13505: Up to 2% Revenue Fine for Failing to Migrate from 1C and BAS
summary: Ukraine's draft law No. 13505 includes a direct financial penalty: a fine of up to 2% of annual revenue for companies that do not complete migration by the set deadlines. Banks and investors already have grounds to refuse financing to companies whose policies reflect continued use of 'hostile software.' Over 300,000 Ukrainian companies are estimated to still run on 1C or BAS.

title: APEXlang in APEX 26.1: ACE Community Explains the DevOps Approach to Low-Code
summary: The Oracle ACE Program published its April 2026 APEXlang must-read roundup. MaxAPEX's blog breaks down the .apx format in depth: a human-readable YAML/JSON for building complete APEX applications with git version control and CI/CD support. APEXlang AI Copilot enables generating and editing applications in natural language. Community consensus: APEX 26.1 is a qualitative leap in DevOps-native low-code development.

title: APEX Alpe Adria 2026: Oracle APEX Conference in Ljubljana, April 16–17
summary: The Oracle APEX community conference organized by Slovenia, Croatia, and Austria takes place April 16–17 at Four Points by Sheraton Ljubljana. Agenda covers APEXlang, new APEX 26.1 features, AI Copilot, and DevOps integration. Part of the growing wave of APEX community events in spring 2026.

title: OCI Autonomous AI Database: Critical Wallet Update Required by April 15
summary: Oracle warns: DigiCert is revoking trust in G1 root certificates on April 15, 2026. Users of Oracle Autonomous AI Database Serverless and Essbase on OCI Marketplace must download a new wallet (issued after January 28, 2026) before the deadline to avoid losing database connectivity.

title: Oracle APEX Bootcamps North America: Free Tour Covering APEX 26.1 & APEXlang
summary: Oracle is running a series of free one-day APEX Bootcamps from April 1 to May 7 across five cities in the US, Canada, and Mexico (Boston, Reston, Minneapolis, Chicago, Guadalajara). Attendees get access to OCI Gen AI and explore APEXlang and the APEX 26.1 approach to AI-driven development.

title: APEX Connect 2026: 12th Annual Oracle APEX Conference at Heide Park Soltau
summary: The 12th edition of APEX Connect takes place May 18, 2026, at Heide Park Soltau, Germany. Organized by DOAG, the leading Oracle community in the DACH region. Topics: APEXlang, AI in APEX, next-generation Interactive Reports, Map Regions, Dynamic Actions, and PL/SQL APIs.

title: Fusion Agentic Applications for HR: 8 AI Agents in Oracle HCM
summary: Oracle announced 8 new AI-agent applications within Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM. Career Advancement Command Center, Contract Compliance Workspace and others automate HR processes — career mobility, contract compliance, and workforce decisions — inside a unified enterprise platform without third-party integrations.

title: Fusion Agentic Applications for Finance & SCM: 12 AI Agents for ERP and Supply Chains
summary: Oracle introduced 12 new AI-agent applications across Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP and SCM. Claims Settlement Workspace accelerates claim resolution and improves financial accuracy. Agents automate financial operations, supply chain management, and manufacturing within a unified agentic ERP stack.

title: OCI Generative AI: Import NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super 120B and Qwen 3 Embedding Models
summary: OCI Generative AI added support for importing open-weights models: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super 120B (high-performance reasoning and instruction following) and Qwen 3 Embedding (text embedding and reranking for enterprise RAG scenarios). Models are available for deployment within a customer's own OCI tenancy.

title: OCI Cache: Valkey 8 with Native JSON and Vector Similarity Search
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Cache announced support for Valkey 8 with improved I/O threading (up to 3.5x faster for KEYS operations and replication), native JSON module, and Valkey-search for high-performance vector similarity search. New capabilities open semantic search scenarios in cache without additional infrastructure.
